articles.title: Anonymous No More: Real-Party Disclosure Comes to Ex Parte Reexamination
articles.content:
作者：Dennis Crouch

美国专利商标局（USPTO）提议终结我们熟知的匿名单方再审程序。定于2026年7月22日在《联邦公报》上公布的拟议规则通知，将增加37 CFR 1.510(b)(7)条款，要求每一第三方单方再审请求必须包含一份声明，识别请求中涉及的所有实际利益方。案卷号PTO-P-2025-0545，RIN 0651-AD94。评议期自公布之日起30天，截止日期约为8月21日。请求人可以要求该声明保密，局方随后将将其从专利文件和再审档案中排除。但保密不同于隐秘。该规则的关键部分在于，即使公众不知道，局方也将知晓每项请求的背后是谁。

双方复审程序一直要求请愿人列明所有实际利益方：法律允许局方"仅在"符合此要求时才考虑请愿书。35 U.S.C. 312(a)(2)。单方再审从未有此要求。第302条允许"任何人"请求再审，第301(e)条允许该人要求将其身份从专利文件中排除并保密。此处的匿名性是国会的设计功能，有时使再审具有吸引力，但该功能似乎将被取消。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

The USPTO has proposed to end anonymous ex parte reexamination as we have known it. A notice of proposed rulemaking scheduled for Federal Register publication on July 22, 2026 would add 37 CFR 1.510(b)(7), requiring every third party request for ex parte reexamination to include a statement identifying all real parties in interest to the request. Docket No. PTO-P-2025-0545, RIN 0651-AD94. Comments are due 30 days after publication, or about August 21. A requester may ask that the statement be kept confidential, and the Office would then exclude it from the patent and reexamination files. But confidential is not the same as secret. A key part of the rule is that the Office would know who is behind a request even when the public does not.

Inter partes review has always demanded that a petitioner name all real parties in interest: the statute lets the Office consider a petition "only if" it does so. 35 U.S.C. 312(a)(2). Ex parte reexamination never has. Section 302 lets "any person" request reexamination, and Section 301(e) lets that person ask to have its identity excluded from the patent file and kept confidential. Anonymity here is a congressional design feature that has sometimes made reexamination attractive, but that feature appears gone.

articles.title: Sedlik en Banc: A Menu for Replacing Copyright’s Intrinsic Test
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇撰稿

第九巡回上诉法院将于2026年9月28日所在周在旧金山进行全席审理，听取关于“内在测试”未来命运的辩论——这是该法院版权侵权两步测试中的主观部分。Sedlik v. Von Drachenberg案，第24-3367号（第九巡回）。这是关于Sedlik案的第二篇文章。六月发布的文章涵盖了全席审理授权以及现已撤销的合议庭判决。法庭之友陈述于本月截止，最后两份（共九份）陈述于7月20日被接受提交，据我阅读，这些陈述为法院提供了五种不同的选项。

法院尚未做的一件事是框定问题。6月9日的再审授权令只有两句话：批准再审，撤销合议庭意见。没有呈述问题，截至本文撰写时也未下令进行补充的实体辩论。因此，选项来自被撤销的并存意见和法庭之友陈述。

案件背景：名人纹身师Kat Von D使用Jeffrey Sedlik 1989年创作的Miles Davis手指抵唇肖像画作为参考，在朋友的胳膊上纹制纹身——用透写台描摹了这张照片，她的店铺在社交媒体上宣传这一成果为“与参考图片100%完全相同”。Sedlik提起侵权诉讼，但陪审团支持Von D。合议庭维持原判，理由是裁决基于内在测试，而该测试本质上不可审查。为纪念此案，大家可以看到我新纹的Von D创作（据称）侵权作品的照片。

被取代的规则。自Sid & Marty Krofft Television Productions v. McDonald's Corp.案，562 F.2d 1157（第九巡回，1977年）以来，第九巡回的版权侵权原告必须两次胜诉：一次在客观的“外在”测试下（逐元素比较、过滤不可保护内容、允许专家证言、作为法律问题可解决），再次在主观的“内在”测试下（普通观察者的整体印象、无专家证言、仅由陪审团决定）。Shaw v. Lindheim案，919 F.2d 1353（第九巡回，1990年）将分析工作转移到了外在部分。内在部分剩余的内容基本上是“整体概念和感觉”的比较：陪审团的“主观判断”，本质上是“缺乏分析”的。第九巡回法院从未在内在测试下推翻过陪审团的不侵权裁决。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

The en banc Ninth Circuit will hear argument during the week of September 28, 2026, in San Francisco, on the future of the "intrinsic test," the subjective half of the circuit's two-part test for copyright infringement. Sedlik v. Von Drachenberg, No. 24-3367 (9th Cir.). This is the second post about Sedlik. The June post covered the en banc grant and the now-vacated panel decision. Amicus briefing wrapped up this month, with the last two of nine briefs accepted for filing on July 20, and my reading is that these set out five distinct options for the court.

One thing the court has not done is frame the question. The June 9 order granting rehearing is two sentences long: rehearing granted, panel opinion vacated. No questions presented, and as of this writing no supplemental merits briefing has been ordered. So the option set comes from the vacated concurrences and the amicus briefs.

The underlying dispute: celebrity tattoo artist Kat Von D used Jeffrey Sedlik's 1989 finger-to-lips portrait of Miles Davis as the reference for a tattoo on a friend's arm, tracing the photograph on a light box, and her shop promoted the result on social media as "100% exactly the same as the reference." Sedlik sued for infringement, but the jury sided with Von D. The panel affirmed on the ground that the verdict rested on the intrinsic test, which is essentially unreviewable. In honor of the case, you can see my new tattoo of Von D creating the (allegedly) infringing work.

The Rule Being Replaced. Since Sid & Marty Krofft Television Productions v. McDonald's Corp., 562 F.2d 1157 (9th Cir. 1977), a Ninth Circuit infringement plaintiff must win twice: once under the objective "extrinsic" test (element-by-element comparison, filtration of unprotectable material, expert testimony allowed, resolvable as a matter of law) and again under the subjective "intrinsic" test (the ordinary observer's overall impression, no experts, jury only). Shaw v. Lindheim, 919 F.2d 1353 (9th Cir. 1990) shifted analytic work into the extrinsic prong. What remained of the intrinsic prong is basically a "total concept and feel" comparison: the "subjective judgment" of the jury that is "virtually devoid of analysis." The Ninth Circuit has never reversed a jury's no-infringement verdict under the intrinsic test.
